etymology.description: 衤(left radical form of 衣(衣) (yī, "clothing")) -> version of 衣 showing depiction of folded or worn garment
etymology.interpretation: 衤 is a variant form of 衣(衣) (yī, "clothing"), representing the appearance of a folded or worn garment, used when part of other characters where the focus is related to clothing or garments.

examples.chinese: 他穿上一件新衤(他穿上一件新衤)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chuānshàng yī jiàn xīn yī.
examples.english: He put on a new outfit.

examples.chinese: 衤字象征着人的外表(衤字象徵著人的外表)。
examples.pinyin: Yī zì xiàngzhēngzhe rén de wàibiǎo.
examples.english: The character 衤 symbolizes the appearance of a person.

examples.chinese: 古代的衤主要用来遮盖身体(古代的衤主要用來遮蓋身體)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de yī zhǔyào yòng lái zhē gài shēntǐ.
examples.english: In ancient times, clothing was mainly used to cover the body.
